title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — Americas — Q2 FY2026 (May – July 2026)
summary: The United States dominates Americas regulatory activity with 16 simultaneous federal and state-level actions converging on stablecoin licensing, AML/CFT modernization, and DeFi enforcement through September 2026. FinCEN's Bank Secrecy Act reform closes for public comment June 9—the most fundamental restructuring of financial institution AML programs in two decades. title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — EMEA — Q2 FY2026 (May – July 2026)
summary: Twenty-one EU member states are executing MiCA implementation while AMLA becomes operational with direct CASP supervision authority—the largest simultaneous regulatory activation in crypto history. UK finalizes its separate authorization gateway while Middle Eastern markets (UAE, Saudi Arabia, Israel) converge on EU-aligned frameworks. This creates immediate compliance infrastructure spend across 200+ authorized CASPs and hundreds more racing toward Q3-Q4 transition deadlines.

title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — Asia-Pacific — Q2 FY2026 (May – July 2026)
summary: Nine Asia-Pacific jurisdictions are simultaneously executing VASP licensing, institutional custody standards, and AML enforcement through September 2026. Australia's AUSTRAC transitions from implementation to active Travel Rule enforcement while Treasury consults on stablecoin payment licensing—the first major market to regulate crypto payment gateways as PSPs.
